_doc: TSBC power-mode rules. The controller (tsbc-power.py, hourly launchd) computes mode and writes .tsbc-power.json; bench.py reads it to cap concurrency + gate heavy tasks. Sprint regime v2 uses six 10h shared-Mac OpCo windows: LOW when 3 overlap or any ad-hoc sprint is active; NORMAL when only 2 overlap.
windowCompanyBasis: Sprint regime v2 (2026-07-07): all six shared-Mac OpCos run 10h staggered windows, so the machine sees alternating 2-active valleys and 3-active overlap blocks all day. LOW now tracks overlap pressure, not the old heavy-hitter subset. Windows still read LIVE from DB so ad-hoc extensions are caught automatically.
maxWorkersNote: normal=2 (conservative: the live fleet + TSMC run 24/7 and heavy fan-out has OOM'd this Mac; bump to 3 if it proves stable). low=1 (zero added pressure).

refresh.note: each suite is re-tested ~monthly; due-day = stable-hash(suite) % staggerOverDays, so suites refresh on different days and never all re-enter testing at once.

sleepDream.note: TSBC is 24/7, not sprint-bound, so it needs a rhythm. DREAM (daily, 06:00-07:00 Dublin, just after the new 05:00 TSK ramp) = self-improvement instead of raw testing: analyse the ledger, refine suites, re-tag skills, fold in real fleet data. SLEEP (weekly, Sun 04-08) intentionally idles through the Sunday 03:30-04:10 backup window and the first 05-07 triple-overlap block. Execution lands with the Drillmaster operator; the controller emits the flags now.
